# misp_noticelist (Resource)

Enable or disable a MISP noticelist. The noticelist definition must already exist on the instance (noticelists are bundled with MISP). Destroying the resource disables the noticelist — it does not remove the definition.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "misp_noticelist" "gdpr" {
  name    = "gdpr"
  enabled = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the noticelist is active on this instance.
- `name` (String) Short noticelist name (e.g. `gdpr`). Replaces the resource if changed.

### Read-Only

- `expanded_name` (String) Human-readable noticelist name.
- `geographical_area` (List of String) Geographical areas covered by this noticelist.
- `id` (String) Numeric MISP identifier.
- `ref` (List of String) Reference URLs for this noticelist.
- `version` (String) Noticelist version string.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

```shell
#!/bin/sh
# Import a MISP noticelist by numeric id. After import, set `name` in your
# config to match what MISP reports for that id.
terraform import misp_noticelist.gdpr 1
```
